title: stramonium vulgare
intro: Stramonium vulgare, also known as common jimsonweed, is a perennial plant in the Solanaceae family, native to the Americas and Europe. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic and expectorant effects, which help to relieve muscle spasms and coughs. The health benefits of Stramonium vulgare include its ability to treat asthma, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiviral agent to combat the common cold and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by drying the leaves and stems, and using them to create a medicinal extract.
